title: Aggregate ReplacingMergeTree table using incremental view
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Question
I have the following table (simplified):
```
CREATE TABLE source
(
client_id Int32,
site_id Int32,
date Date32,
visitors Int64,
_peerdb_synced_at DateTime64(9) DEFAULT now64(),
_peerdb_is_deleted UInt8,
_peerdb_version Int64
)
ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(_peerdb_version, _peerdb_is_deleted)
PRIMARY KEY (client_id, site_id, date)
ORDER BY (client_id, site_id, date)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
```
I want to preaggregate the data - sum the number of visitors per client_id and date. The problem is that existing records may change. Since I'm using clickpipes I'll just get a new row with higher version but I can't use SummingMergeTree (since the old value won't be subtracted from the sum).
So I came up with the following solution:
```
CREATE TABLE target
(
client_id Int32,
date Date32,
visitors Int64,
deleted UInt8,
version Int64
)
ENGINE = ReplacingMergeTree(version, deleted)
PRIMARY KEY (client_id, date)
ORDER BY (client_id, date)
SETTINGS index_granularity = 8192;
```
```
CREATE VIEW source_final AS SELECT * FROM source FINAL;
CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW source_mv TO target AS
SELECT
part.client_id as client_id,
part.date as date,
full.visitors AS visitors,
now64() AS version,
if(full.client_id = 0, 1, 0) AS deleted
FROM (SELECT DISTINCT ON (client_id, date) client_id, date FROM source) as part
LEFT JOIN (
SELECT
client_id,
date,
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE source_test (client_id INT, site_id INT, date DATE, visitors BIGINT, _peerdb_synced_at DATETIME DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP, _peerdb_is_deleted TINYINT DEFAULT 0, _peerdb_version BIGINT) ENGINE=InnoDB;
INSERT INTO source_test (client_id, site_id, date, visitors, _peerdb_version) VALUES (1, 100, '2023-10-01', 50, 1);
INSERT INTO source_test (client_id, site_id, date, visitors, _peerdb_version) VALUES (1, 100, '2023-10-01', 70, 2);
CREATE TABLE target_test (client_id INT, date DATE, visitors BIGINT, deleted TINYINT, version BIGINT);
INSERT INTO target_test (client_id, date, visitors, deleted, version) SELECT client_id, date, SUM(visitors), 0, MAX(_peerdb_version) FROM source_test GROUP BY client_id, date;
SELECT * FROM target_test WHERE visitors > 0 ORDER BY client_id, date;
key_bug_features: ReplacingMergeTree with incremental view for data aggregation
